key_insight: Single-cell profiling of >85,000 RCC cells uncovered 59 distinct gene programs that refine subtype identity and reveal transcriptional states linked to metastasis and poor outcomes.
clinical_relevance: These findings may improve RCC subtype classification, highlight biomarkers such as CASP14 for rare clear cell papillary tumors, and suggest transcriptional signatures for identifying aggressive disease.
method: Profiled primary, metastatic, and patient-derived RCC samples across four subtypes using single-cell gene expression sequencing.
Applied a generative modeling framework to separate gene programs from clonal and copy-number-driven expression effects.
Mapped program activity to regulators, spatial organization, and clinical outcome associations.
evidence_signal: The study compiled more than 85,000 single-cell expression profiles and used them to define 59 gene expression programs across RCC models and patient tumors.
watch_item: These are discovery-stage transcriptomic associations, so biomarker and signature performance will need independent validation in larger, prospective cohorts before clinical use.
paper_title: Single-cell gene programs define subtype identity and metastatic trajectories in renal cell carcinoma
